MINUTES FOR AUG-02-2017 9:30 AM

LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENTS JERRY BROWN, RICHARD GILLIHAN, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN RESOURCES, MARYBEL BATJER, CALIFORNIA GOVERNMENT OPERATIONS AGENCY, NANCY BARGMANN, AND CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF DEVELOPMENTAL SERVICES' MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADING IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE FOR PETITIONER'S FAILURE TO EXHAUST ITS ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDY, BUT WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO PETITIONER REFILING IT AFTER PERB HAS MADE A FINAL DETERMINATION OF THE PERB COMPLAINT, AND THE COURT STAYS THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF PENDING PERB'S FINAL DETERMINATION OF THE PERB COMPLAINT. (SEE FRESNO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT V. NATIONAL EDUCATION ASSOCIATION (1981) 125 CAL.APP.3D 259.) THE COURT GRANTS RESPONDENTS' REQUESTS FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE AS TO THE FOLLOWING: (1) EX. A [PERB COMPLAINT]; (2) EX. B [UNFAIR PRACTICE CHARGE]; (3) EX. F [OPERATIVE MOU]; AND (4) EX. I [NOTICE OF FORMAL HEARING]. (SEE EVIDENCE CODE SEC. 452(C).) THE COURT READS ITS FINDINGS AND REASONS IN OPEN COURT WITH COUNSEL FOR BOTH SIDES PRESENT AS REFLECTED IN THE TRANSCRIPT OF THE CERTIFIED REPORTER AND THE CLERK'S .DOC (WORD) MINUTES. A COPY OF THE COURT'S FULL RULING IS HAND-DELIVERED TO COUNSEL IN OPEN COURT. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ANGELA BRADSTREET; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: MARIA VISCIO, CSR NO. 12353, EMAIL: MVISCIO@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

calendar.matters:
Respondents Motion For Judgment On The Pleading
Off Calendar Aug-02-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Will Yamada of the law offices of Tribuiano | Yamda, LLP (916-333-500) appears as counsel for petitioner/opposing party

Attorney, David M. Villalba, Labor Relations Counsel of State of California, Department of Human Resources (916-324-0512) appears as counsel for respondent/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

RESPONDENTS JERRY BROWN, RICHARD GILLIHAN, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN RESOURCES, MARYBEL BATJER, CALIFORNIA GOVERNMENT OPERATIONS AGENCY, NANCY BARGMANN, and CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF DEVELOPMENTAL SERVICES' Motion For Judgment On The Pleading is GRANTED without leave to amend as to the first cause of action for writ of mandate for Petitioner's failure to exhaust its administrative remedy, but without prejudice to Petitioner refiling it after PERB has made a final determination of the PERB complaint, and the Court STAYS the second cause of action for declaratory relief pending PERB's final determination of the PERB complaint. (See Fresno Unified School District v. National Education Association (1981) 125 Cal.App.3d 259.) The Court grants Respondents' requests for judicial notice as to the following: (1) Ex. A [PERB complaint]; (2) Ex. B [unfair practice charge]; (3) Ex. F [operative MOU]; and (4) Ex. I [notice of formal hearing]. (See Evidence Code sec. 452(c).) The Court reads its findings and reasons in open court with counsel for both sides present as reflected in the transcript of the certified reporter and the Clerk's .doc (Word) minutes. A copy of the Court's full ruling is hand-delivered to counsel in open court. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Angela Bradstreet; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Viscio, CSR No. 12353, email: mviscio@sftc.org. (D302)
